summary: Build a chat/messaging social app similar to WhatsApp and Instagram.
known_issues: Users are unable to follow other users (follow/unfollow functionality not working in the current deployed build).

existing_features.title: Implement Internet Identity authentication and a minimal onboarding flow: first sign-in creates a user record; prompt for display name, unique username/handle, and optional bio + avatar.

Create core data models and canister methods for a simple Instagram-like social layer: user search, follow/unfollow, and a posts feed where users can create posts with a caption and up to 10 images (stored as frontend static asset URLs) and view a timeline feed from followed users.

Create a WhatsApp-like 1:1 direct messaging system: start a conversation with another user, list conversations, send messages, and view message history with polling-based refresh (no real-time sockets).
Add navigation and app structure for the MVP with these primary sections: Feed, Explore, Messages, Profile, and Create Post; ensure consistent layout across desktop and mobile widths.
Choose and apply a coherent visual theme for the entire UI (colors, typography, spacing, and component styling) suitable for a modern chat + social app; enforce it consistently across all screens.
Add basic safety/quality features: input validation and empty/loading/error states across Feed, Explore, Messages, and Profile.
Wire Explore page follow/unfollow to the backend using Principals from search results, and remove the current disabled follow/unfollow UI state.
Enable “Start chat” from Explore by using the searched user’s Principal ID, consistent with how startConversation expects a Principal.

new_feature_requests.summary: Create a WhatsApp/Instagram-like chatting app (messaging + social features).

Ensure stable canister storage for users, follows, posts, conversations, and messages using the Internet Computer canister state patterns appropriate for upgrades (stable storage) within a single Motoko actor.
Update the backend user search API so the frontend can identify each search result by Principal, enabling follow/unfollow calls that require a Principal.
